methods.description: This method involves the detailed logging and analysis of stratigraphic sections to study stratal geometries, stacking patterns, and facies variations. It is used to understand the depositional environment and geological history of the area.

steps.description: Fluvial incision has revealed laterally continuous outcrops oriented subparallel and subperpendicular to the depositional strike that enabled the stratal geometries and stacking patterns to be studied.
steps.searchable_summary: study stratal geometries and stacking patterns from outcrops

steps.description: Clinoforms and clinothems were mapped using outcrop panoramic photomosaics while stratigraphic contacts and facies were checked in the field.
steps.searchable_summary: map clinoforms and clinothems using photomosaics

steps.description: Two main sections, 44 m thick (Cabezo Alto) and 77 m thick (Cañada Blanca), were logged in detail, for lithology, sedimentary structures, macrofossil composition, biofabrics and ichnoassemblages to evaluate the vertical variation and stacking of facies.
steps.searchable_summary: log sections for lithology and facies analysis

steps.description: The macrofauna was identified to species level whenever possible, except for most bryozoans, for which only the zooarial morphology was noted.
The abundance of macrofaunal taxa was estimated in the field by distinguishing between dominant (26 to 100%), common (11 to 25%) and rare (1 to 10%) categories.
Some bulk samples of uncemented sediment were sieved through 500 lm, 125 lm and 63 lm meshes to explore qualitatively the content of benthic and planktonic foraminifera in the 125 lm fraction.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve sediment samples for foraminifera content

methods.description: This method measures the magnetic susceptibility of rock samples as a proxy for terrigenous input, providing insights into sedimentary processes and environmental conditions.

steps.description: Magnetic susceptibility was measured in the field, with a SM-20 magnetic susceptibility meter for the Cabezo Alto and Cañada Blanca sections.
Five to six replicate measurements per sampling site were taken on flat rock surfaces, and the mean value reported.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate mean magnetic susceptibility from replicates

methods.description: This method quantifies the carbonate content of rock samples using a Scheibler calcimeter, following standardized procedures to assess sediment composition.

steps.description: Carbonate content was quantified with a Scheibler calcimeter for samples in the Cabezo Alto and Cañada Blanca sections.
The procedure specified in ISO 10693:1995 has been followed.
steps.searchable_summary: follow ISO 10693:1995 procedure for carbonate quantification

methods.description: This method categorizes the maturity of glauconite based on K2O content and color, using electron probe microanalysis to determine its composition and maturity stage.

steps.description: Glauconite maturity has been categorized into four stages, based on the K2O content.
steps.searchable_summary: categorize glauconite maturity by K2O content

steps.description: Glauconite composition was examined using a Cameca SXFive FE Electron Probe Microanalyzer.
For matrix corrections, the PAP method was applied to all acquired data.
steps.searchable_summary: apply PAP method for matrix corrections
